tools.name: document_open
tools.description: Open an existing document in the editor panel. Use this when the user asks to see or pull up a document that exists but isn't currently visible in the editor — for example after switching devices, refreshing, or when the editor panel was closed.

surface_id.description: The ID of the document to open

tools.name: document_create
tools.description: Create a new long-form document with a rich text editor. Use this when the user asks to write a blog post, article, or any long-form content. The editor opens in workspace mode with chat docked to the side.

title.description: Initial title for the document (optional, can be updated later)

initial_content.description: Initial Markdown content to populate the editor (optional)

tools.name: document_update
tools.description: Append to or fully replace a document's content. Takes the full Markdown in `content` plus a `mode`: "append" adds it to the end (use this to stream generated content in chunks), "replace" overwrites the entire document. This tool does NOT do targeted edits — for changing specific words, phrases, or sections, use document_replace_text instead. There is no old/new or find/replace parameter here.

surface_id.description: The ID of the document surface to update

content.description: Markdown content to set or append

mode.description: Whether to replace all content or append to the end. Defaults to append.

tools.name: document_read
tools.description: Read the current content of a document by its surface_id when it belongs to the current conversation, or when the current actor is the guardian/local user. Use this to verify document state before making edits.

surface_id.description: The ID of the document to read

tools.name: document_list
tools.description: List documents. Without a query, lists documents in the current conversation. With a query, searches documents by title; guardian/local users can search across conversations, while other actors are scoped to the current conversation.

query.description: Search documents by title. Omit to list only the current conversation's documents.

tools.name: document_delete
tools.description: Delete a document by its surface_id.

surface_id.description: The ID of the document to delete

tools.name: document_find
tools.description: Search for text or a regex pattern within a document. Returns matching lines with line numbers and context — like grep. Use this to locate specific content before making targeted edits with document_replace_text.

query.description: Text or regex pattern to search for

regex.description: Treat query as a regex pattern. Defaults to false (literal match).

tools.name: document_replace_text
tools.description: Find and replace text within a document — like sed. Performs targeted replacements without rewriting the entire document. Supports literal text and regex patterns. Use document_find first to preview what will be matched.

find.description: Text or regex pattern to find

replace.description: Replacement text. When using regex, supports backreferences ($1, $2, etc.).

regex.description: Treat find as a regex pattern. Defaults to false (literal match).

max_replacements.description: Maximum number of replacements to make. Omit to replace all occurrences.

tools.name: comment_list
tools.description: List open comments on a document. Returns all unresolved comments with their content, anchor text, and thread context so you can address user feedback.

tools.name: comment_resolve
tools.description: Mark a comment as resolved after you have addressed the feedback. Use this after editing the document to satisfy the comment.

tools.name: comment_reply
tools.description: Reply to a comment thread. Use this to ask clarifying questions or explain your approach before or after making changes.

comment_id.description: The parent comment ID to reply to
